title: tasks[].phase carries free-text sub-phase labels (e.g. "8.7.1") instead of FK references to PHASE-NNN phase block items
description: .project/tasks.json items use task.phase as a free-text string holding sub-phase labels from the canonical FGAP-026 sub-phase decomposition (e.g. TASK-031/032/033 carry phase: "8.7.1"). The substrate also defines a phase block kind (PHASE-NNN id pattern via packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/phase.schema.json) whose items would be the canonical FK target. Currently phase block items for the FGAP-026 closure plan are not populated — HANDOFF documents PHASE-006..010 as scheduled for Phase 5 of the FGAP-026 closure plan (the arc-tracking-blocks dogfood deliverable per TASK-025). Until Phase 5 lands the PHASE-006..010 substrate, every task.phase field carrying a sub-phase label is a free-text placeholder rather than a typed reference. Per DEC-0013 (closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships), the proper representation is either (a) PHASE-NNN id as FK string OR (b) closure-table edge with relation_type = belongs_to_phase. Free-text labels are neither.

evidence.reference: TASK-031/032/033 carry phase: "8.7.1" — free-text sub-phase label, not a PHASE-NNN FK; same shape for TASK-021..030 carrying phase: "8.7.1" through "8.7.10"
Phase block schema exists; PHASE-NNN id pattern; ready to receive the FGAP-026 closure plan phase items
HANDOFF documents PHASE-006..010 as scheduled for Phase 5 of FGAP-026 closure (TASK-025 arc-tracking-blocks dogfood deliverable)
DEC-0013 closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships — free-text phase labels violate this

impact: DEC-0013 canon violation: tasks[].phase carries free-text labels where the canon mandates closure-table edges for inter-item relationships. Validators surfacing the gap correctly flag tasks[].phase as unresolvable references; cross-block phase queries (e.g. all tasks in PHASE-007) cannot run; status-by-phase rollup (per roadmap-plan PM-lens semantics) misses these tasks until phase substrate is populated. Every task added during the FGAP-026 closure carries the same free-text shape; migration scope grows with each filing.
proposed_resolution: Resolved by Phase 5 (TASK-025) execution: ship PHASE-006..010 phase items + relations.json edges (task → phase via relation_type = belongs_to_phase per DEC-0013 closure-table-only convention) + migration via schema-migrations.ts rewriting existing task.phase free-text labels into typed relations.json edges. tasks schema deprecates the inline phase field (or removes it) once relations.json carries the references. Substantive sub-gap of FGAP-026 phase 5 — fix vehicle is TASK-025 execution, not a parallel arc.
